pageHeader.title: Books
pageHeader.subtitle: Scholarly Works on the Mahabharata
pageHeader.description: Explore my published books that bring rigorous scholarship and fresh perspectives on the Mahabharata to both academic and general audiences.

books.title: The Mahabharata: A Modern Reader's Companion
books.description: A comprehensive guide that navigates readers through the vast narrative landscape of the Mahabharata. This companion breaks down the epic's complex structure, introduces key characters, explains major philosophical concepts, and provides contextual background for each book (parva) of the epic. Ideal for students, scholars, and general readers seeking a reliable entry point into Mahabharata studies.

books.title: Dharma in Dialogue: Ethical Frameworks from the Mahabharata
books.description: An in-depth exploration of the ethical debates embedded within the Mahabharata. This book examines how the epic presents dharma not as a fixed code but as a dynamic, context-dependent framework for ethical decision-making. Through close readings of key episodes — the dice game, the war council, and the final discourse — the book reveals the Mahabharata's sophisticated engagement with moral philosophy.

books.title: Women of the Mahabharata: Voices from the Epic
books.description: A groundbreaking study that centers the experiences and voices of women in the Mahabharata — Draupadi, Kunti, Gandhari, Subhadra, and others. This work challenges traditional interpretations and reveals the complexity, agency, and philosophical contributions of these characters beyond their conventional roles.

seo.title: Books by Varun Gupta | Mahabharata Scholarship
seo.description: Browse books by Varun Gupta on the Mahabharata — scholarly works that decode the epic's wisdom, philosophy, and narratives for modern readers.
